content:
# Final Review Votes
**Producer:** agent:claude/claude-opus-4-6

## Summary

All three amendments identify real issues in the success reconciliation code. Amendment fr-17-2f3210bd (persist run_id) is a genuine correctness bug — the most critical of the three. Amendment fr-17-a299d694 (bypass worktree for reconciliation retries) is a real robustness defect confirmed by code inspection. Amendment fr-17-dde66e40 (RunResumed fallback) is the weakest — the code already has an explicit comment (lines 1687-1691) explaining why RunResumed timestamps are not used, but adding a defensive fallback for run_id extraction (distinct from timestamp handling) is still reasonable hardening.

## Votes

- `fr-17-dde66e40`: Accept
  Code inspection confirms try_reconcile_success (line 1675) only searches for RunStarted. However, lines 1687-1691 already contain an explicit comment explaining that RunResumed has a different timestamp that conflicts with lineage validation — partially addressing this amendment's concern about documentation. The amendment's option (a) is largely already done. Option (b) — using RunResumed as a fallback for run_id extraction specifically — is still valid as cheap defensive coding. The run_id itself (as opposed to the timestamp) is the same across RunStarted and RunResumed for the same run, so using RunResumed as a fallback for run_id extraction would not introduce the timestamp mismatch the existing comment warns about. Accept as minor hardening, but note that the existing code is already partially documented and functionally correct for append-only journals.
- `fr-17-a299d694`: Accept
  Code inspection confirms this is a real robustness defect. The call chain is: retry_task → task becomes Pending → daemon picks it up → claim_task calls LeaseService::acquire (line 157-168 in task_service.rs) → worktree created (lease_service.rs line 167) and try_resume_from_remote attempted (line 216) → THEN claim_task returns → THEN is_reconciliation_retry guard checked (daemon_loop.rs lines 686-696, 1436-1446). For reconciliation-only retries (BrCloseFailed, BrSyncFailed, MilestoneUpdateFailed), the only work needed is br close/sync plus milestone bookkeeping — none of which requires a worktree. Yet the current flow creates a worktree and attempts remote branch resume, either of which can fail due to disk, network, or auth issues, causing the retry to fail before reaching try_reconcile_success. The failure_class should be inspected before or during claim_task to skip worktree operations for reconciliation-specific retries.
- `fr-17-2f3210bd`: Accept
  This is the most critical amendment — a genuine correctness bug. Code inspection confirms DaemonTask (model.rs) stores only project_id and requirements_run_id with no workflow run_id field. try_reconcile_success (line 1675) reconstructs the run_id by finding the most recent RunStarted in the project journal. If an operator manually reruns or resumes the project between the original reconciliation failure and the retry_task call, a new RunStarted event will be appended to the journal. The reconciliation code will then pick up the wrong run_id, potentially writing completion timestamps for the wrong run, attaching the task to the wrong workflow execution, and performing lineage repair against unrelated data. This is not theoretical — operator intervention between failure and retry is exactly when reconciliation retries occur. Storing the workflow run_id on DaemonTask at dispatch time and reading it in try_reconcile_success is the correct fix.
